entities.name: Fitbit
entities.context: Health data source used for syncing workout and intraday activity data

entities.name: Workout Tracker
entities.context: Internal system component (workout_tracker) that receives and stores synced fitness data

entities.name: Intraday Sync
entities.context: Process (_sync_intraday_for_date) that pulls granular intraday health metrics from Fitbit per date

entities.name: Activity Import Pipeline
entities.context: Per-activity workout import flow that parses Fitbit activity logs into a normalized payload schema

entities.name: Normalized Workout Payload
entities.context: Standardized data structure with fields: date, start_time, modality, duration_min, distance_km, calories, avg_hr, source, external_id, raw_payload

relationships.context: Fitbit is the external data source; Workout Tracker is the local store receiving synced data
Intraday sync process calls Fitbit API via auth token to retrieve per-date granular data
Pipeline consumes Fitbit activity log entries (logId, startTime, duration, distance, calories)
Pipeline transforms raw Fitbit activity records into the normalized payload schema
Normalized payloads are the interface format written into the workout tracker database
